message.content: {"items":[{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2010","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11559","justification":"The 5th European Working Conditions Survey (EWCS 2010) includes detailed data on working conditions for many European countries (including Belgium, Denmark, Germany, Greece, Spain, Finland, France, Ireland, Italy, Luxembourg, Netherlands, Austria, Portugal, Sweden, United Kingdom, Bulgaria, Cyprus, Czech Republic, Estonia, Hungary, Latvia, Lithuania, Malta, Poland, Romania, Slovakia, Slovenia, and others). It is archived by GESIS for the year 2010, fitting the 2010s period and multi-country scope requested."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2015","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12660","justification":"The 6th EWCS (2015) provides harmonized information on working conditions in EU Member States and associated countries, including most of the countries listed by the user. It is a GESIS-hosted dataset covering mid‑2010s working conditions and is specifically designed for cross-country comparisons."},{"title":"European Working Conditions Survey 2013 (Integrated Data File, 1991-2013)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11831","justification":"The integrated EWCS 1991–2013 file (including the 2010 and 2013 waves) allows analysis of working conditions over time and across many European countries listed in the query, and is curated by GESIS. It covers the early 2010s and enables multi-year comparisons within the decade."},{"title":"Quality of Work and Employment in Europe (EWCS 2010, 2015, 2005, 2000, 1995) – Cumulative File","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13670","justification":"This cumulative GESIS file combines several EWCS waves (including 2010 and 2015) into one dataset focused on quality of work and employment. It covers working conditions across numerous European countries in the 2010s and is tailored to comparative analyses across time and countries."},{"title":"European Company Survey 2013","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11988","justification":"Although employer‑side, the European Company Survey 2013 (ECS) contains rich information on work organization, HR practices, and working time arrangements across many EU countries and some non‑EU ones. Archived by GESIS, it allows analysis of working conditions context in the early 2010s for countries such as Belgium, Denmark, Germany, Spain, Finland, France, Ireland, Italy, Netherlands, Austria, Portugal, Sweden, United Kingdom, and others."},{"title":"European Company Survey 2019","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13624","justification":"ECS 2019, archived at GESIS, provides information on work organization, employee participation, and workplace practices that directly affect working conditions in European countries, including many from the user’s list. As it refers to 2019 fieldwork, it still belongs to the 2010s decade and complements person‑level EWCS data with establishment‑level information."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2010 (Round 5)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11159","justification":"ESS Round 5 includes modules on work, family, and well‑being, with variables on job security, work–life balance, and related aspects of working conditions. It covers many of the listed European countries and is hosted by GESIS, fitting the early 2010s period."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2012 (Round 6)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.11648","justification":"ESS Round 6 contains repeated questions on employment conditions (e.g., contract type, working hours, job security) across numerous European countries including many in the user’s list. The data are archived at GESIS and relate to mid‑2010s working conditions."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2014 (Round 7)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.12253","justification":"ESS Round 7, hosted by GESIS, provides cross‑national microdata for many European countries, including key variables on employment status, contract type, working time, and job security. This allows analysis of working conditions across the user’s listed countries in the mid‑2010s."},{"title":"European Social Survey 2016 (Round 8)","url_or_doi":"https://doi.org/10.4232/1.13326","justification":"ESS Round 8 contains harmonized data on employment and job characteristics for many European countries, including several from the user’s list (e.g., Germany, Spain, France, UK, Sweden, Norway). Archived by GESIS, it provides late‑2010s information relevant to comparative research on working conditions."}]}
